leagueBriefing: It was a decisive Sunday in the Bundesliga's survival scrap as Werder Bremen emphatically climbed out of the drop zone with a dominant display in the capital. While St. Pauli and Eintracht Frankfurt neutralized each other in a tactical stalemate in Hamburg, Bremen took full advantage of a numerical edge in Berlin to secure back-to-back wins for the first time this season. The results provide a massive boost to Bremen’s safety hopes while leaving Union Berlin and St. Pauli looking nervously over their shoulders.

games.headline: Werder Bremen thrashes 10-man Union to escape relegation zone
games.brief: Werder Bremen's survival bid received a massive injection of life as they came from behind to dismantle Union Berlin at the Alte Försterei. The match turned on its head in the 19th minute when Union's András Schäfer was shown a straight red card just moments after Derrick Köhn had put the hosts ahead from the penalty spot. With a man advantage, Bremen took total control; Olivier Deman and Jens Stage struck before the break, and a late debut goal from 18-year-old Patrice Čović put the exclamation point on a victory that lifts Bremen to 13th in the table.
bullets: András Schäfer was sent off in the 19th minute for a dangerous tackle on Jens Stage, a decision that derailed Union's early momentum.
Midfielder Jens Stage was named Man of the Match, scoring the go-ahead goal and recording a game-high 19 pressure situations.
Patrice Čović, son of former Hertha Berlin coach Ante Čović, scored his first Bundesliga goal with his very first touch after entering in stoppage time.
Bremen dominated possession (74.5%) and pass accuracy (90%) following the red card to secure their first away win under Daniel Thioune.
Union Berlin has now managed only one victory in the 2026 calendar year, leaving them just three points clear of the relegation play-off spot.
games.context: The 4-1 rout sees Werder Bremen jump three places to 13th, while Union Berlin remains mired in a relegation fight.

highlights.title: Bremen Leaves Relegation Zone! | UNION BERLIN - WERDER BREMEN | Highlights | Bundesliga

games.headline: Woodwork denies St. Pauli as Frankfurt holds firm for goalless draw
games.brief: St. Pauli and Eintracht Frankfurt played out a high-intensity but ultimately scoreless draw at the Millerntor-Stadion. Despite Frankfurt dominating nearly 73% of the ball, it was the hosts who came closest to finding a winner, striking the woodwork twice in a frantic seven-minute span during the first half through Mathias Pereira Lage and Danel Sinani. Frankfurt’s defense, led by a standout performance from Aurèle Amenda, successfully weathered the storm to earn a clean sheet, but the point wasn't enough to prevent St. Pauli from sliding back into the relegation play-off spot following results elsewhere.
bullets: St. Pauli hit the post twice in the first half: Mathias Pereira Lage in the 24th minute and Danel Sinani in the 31st.
Eintracht Frankfurt controlled 72.5% of possession and completed 679 passes compared to St. Pauli’s 243.
Frankfurt defender Aurèle Amenda earned Man of the Match honors with 12 challenges won and an 89% pass completion rate.
St. Pauli goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj made several key interventions, including a sharp save against Jean-Mattéo Bahoya early in the second half.
The draw extends Frankfurt coach Albert Riera's wait for his first Bundesliga victory on the road.
games.context: St. Pauli drops to 16th (the relegation play-off spot) despite the point, while Frankfurt remains in 7th place.

highlights.title: ST. PAULI - EINTRACHT FRANKFURT | Highlights | Matchday 25 – Bundesliga 2025/26
